issues: Draft says 'decisive third period' implying all Richmond goals were scored in the third, but the OCR states the Sockeyes led 2-0 after the first period and 2-1 after the second — only Rutherford's winner and Tomlinson's goal came in the third.
Draft omits Humboldt scorers Curtis Chamberlin and Kevin Luke, which are present in the OCR.

Draft omits the shot disparity context (46 shots faced by Romeo vs. only 23 by Humboldt), though Romeo's shot count is mentioned — the framing in the draft ('stopping 46 shots') is accurate but the broader context is missing.
Draft describes the series as a 'four-team series' which matches OCR ('four-team, round-robin series') — this is fine.

Minor: draft says 'Rutherford and Dave Tomlinson also scored for Richmond in the decisive third period' — Rutherford scored the winner in the third, but the article does not explicitly place Tomlinson's goal in the third period either.
review.correctedDescriptionShort: Jason Phillips scored three goals as the Richmond Sockeyes defeated the Humboldt Broncos 5-2 to win the Centennial Cup junior-A championship on May 9, 1987, in Humboldt, Saskatchewan.
review.correctedDescriptionLong: Jason Phillips scored three goals to lead the Richmond Sockeyes to a 5-2 victory over the Humboldt Broncos in Humboldt, Saskatchewan on May 9, 1987, claiming the Centennial Cup junior-A championship. Goaltender Frank Romeo was named MVP of the four-team round-robin series after facing 46 shots while his teammates managed only 23. Paul Rutherford scored the winner in the third period and Dave Tomlinson also scored for Richmond; Curtis Chamberlin and Kevin Luke replied for the Saskatchewan champion Broncos.
review.notes: Draft incorrectly attributed all scoring to the third period; the OCR shows Richmond held leads of 2-0 and 2-1 before the third. Humboldt scorers were also omitted. Corrected long description restores accurate game flow and includes all scorers per the OCR.
